title: Components
description: Holds a set of reusable objects for different aspects of the OAS.

__schema1.title: Schema Object
__schema1.description: A Schema Object defines the shape of a JSON value.

__schema2.title: Reference Object
__schema2.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

examples.description: A user is a type of person.
A pet is a type of animal.
A pet is a type of animal.

__schema3.title: Reference
__schema3.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

__schema7.title: summary
__schema7.description: A short summary which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema8.title: description
__schema8.description: A description which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema10.title: String Object
__schema10.description: A String Object defines the shape of a string value.

__schema11.title: title
__schema11.description: A title for the schema, used for documentation.

__schema12.title: description
__schema12.description: A description of the schema. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema13.title: default
__schema13.description: The default value for this schema.

__schema14.title: example
__schema14.description: An example value for this schema.

__schema17.title: enum
__schema17.description: An array of valid values for this schema.

__schema19.title: const
__schema19.description: A constant value that this schema must match.

__schema21.title: Discriminator
__schema21.description: When request bodies or response payloads may be one of a number of different schemas, a discriminator object can be used to aid in serialization, deserialization, and validation.

__schema22.title: propertyName
__schema22.description: The name of the property in the payload that will hold the discriminator value.

__schema23.title: mapping
__schema23.description: An object to hold mappings between payload values and schema names or references.

__schema26.title: defaultMapping
__schema26.description: The default mapping to use when no matching discriminator value is found.

__schema29.title: XML
__schema29.description: A metadata object that allows for more fine-tuned XML model definitions.

__schema30.title: name
__schema30.description: Replaces the name of the element/attribute used for the described schema property.

__schema31.title: namespace
__schema31.description: The URI of the namespace definition.

__schema32.title: prefix
__schema32.description: The prefix to be used for the name.

__schema33.description: Declares whether the property definition translates to an attribute instead of an element.
__schema33.title: attribute

__schema35.description: May be used only for an array definition. Signifies whether the array is wrapped or not.
__schema35.title: wrapped

__schema39.title: ExternalDocumentation
__schema39.description: Allows referencing an external resource for extended documentation.

__schema40.title: description
__schema40.description: A short description of the target documentation.

__schema41.title: url
__schema41.description: The URL for the target documentation.

__schema43.title: readOnly
__schema43.description: When true, the property is only returned in responses, not accepted in requests.

__schema44.title: writeOnly
__schema44.description: When true, the property is only accepted in requests, not returned in responses.

__schema45.title: deprecated
__schema45.description: When true, indicates this schema is deprecated and should be avoided.

__schema47.title: format
__schema47.description: Semantic format hint for string values. Common formats: date-time, email, uuid, uri, hostname, ipv4, byte, binary.

__schema48.title: Pattern
__schema48.description: A regular expression pattern the string must match.

__schema54.title: Number Object
__schema54.description: A Number Object defines the shape of a number value.

__schema56.title: format
__schema56.description: Number precision format. Use 'float' for 32-bit single-precision or 'double' (default) for 64-bit double-precision IEEE 754.

__schema57.title: multipleOf
__schema57.description: Require the number to be a multiple of this value.

__schema59.title: minimum
__schema59.description: Inclusive lower bound for the number.

__schema61.title: maximum
__schema61.description: Inclusive upper bound for the number.

__schema63.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema63.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema65.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema65.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema67.title: Integer Object
__schema67.description: An Integer Object defines the shape of an integer value.

__schema69.title: format
__schema69.description: Integer size format. Use 'int32' for signed 32-bit (-2B to 2B) or 'int64' for signed 64-bit integers.

__schema70.title: multipleOf
__schema70.description: Require the integer to be a multiple of this value.

__schema72.title: minimum
__schema72.description: Inclusive lower bound for the integer.

__schema74.title: maximum
__schema74.description: Inclusive upper bound for the integer.

__schema76.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema76.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema78.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema78.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema80.title: Boolean Object
__schema80.description: A Boolean Object defines the shape of a boolean value.

__schema82.title: Null Object
__schema82.description: A Null Object defines the shape of a null value.

__schema84.title: Array Object
__schema84.description: An Array Object defines the shape of an array value.

__schema88.title: prefixItems
__schema88.description: Tuple validation: schemas for items by index (array position).

__schema90.title: contains
__schema90.description: Require the array to contain at least one item matching this subschema.

__schema102.title: Object Object
__schema102.description: An Object Object defines the shape of an object value.

__schema126.title: Composition Schema
__schema126.description: A schema using composition keywords (allOf, anyOf, oneOf, not, if/then/else) without a type constraint.

__schema127.title: allOf
__schema127.description: Require the instance to validate against all subschemas.

__schema129.title: anyOf
__schema129.description: Require the instance to validate against at least one subschema.

__schema131.title: oneOf
__schema131.description: Require the instance to validate against exactly one subschema.

__schema133.title: not
__schema133.description: Require the instance to NOT validate against the given subschema.

__schema135.title: if
__schema135.description: Conditional validation: if the instance matches this schema, apply then; otherwise apply else.

__schema139.title: else
__schema139.description: Subschema applied when if does not match.

__schema142.title: Response
__schema142.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema143.title: Response
__schema143.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema147.title: Header
__schema147.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema148.title: Header
__schema148.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema149.title: description
__schema149.description: A brief description of the parameter.

__schema150.description: Determines whether this parameter is mandatory.
__schema150.title: required

__schema152.description: Specifies that a parameter is deprecated.
__schema152.title: deprecated

__schema154.description: Sets the ability to pass empty-valued parameters.
__schema154.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema156.title: style
__schema156.description: Describes how the parameter value will be serialized.

__schema171.title: externalValue
__schema171.description: A URL that points to the literal example.

__schema172.title: dataValue
__schema172.description: The data value of the example before serialization. Mutually exclusive with value and externalValue.

__schema173.title: serializedValue
__schema173.description: The serialized representation of the example. Mutually exclusive with value and externalValue.

__schema181.title: MediaType
__schema181.description: Each Media Type Object provides schema and examples for the media type identified by its key.

__schema189.title: Encoding
__schema189.description: A single encoding definition applied to a single schema property.

__schema190.title: contentType
__schema190.description: The Content-Type for encoding a specific property.

__schema203.title: itemSchema
__schema203.description: Schema for individual items in streaming responses.

__schema204.title: itemEncoding
__schema204.description: Encoding for individual items in streaming responses.

__schema209.title: Link
__schema209.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema210.title: Link
__schema210.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema211.title: operationId
__schema211.description: The name of an existing, resolvable OAS operation, as defined with a unique operationId.

__schema212.title: operationRef
__schema212.description: A relative or absolute URI reference to an OAS operation.

__schema213.title: parameters
__schema213.description: A map representing parameters to pass to an operation as specified with operationId or identified via operationRef.

__schema216.title: requestBody
__schema216.description: A literal value or {expression} to use as a request body when calling the target operation.

__schema219.title: Server
__schema219.description: An object representing a Server. OpenAPI 3.2 adds 'name' for identification.

__schema220.title: url
__schema220.description: A URL to the target host. Supports server variables in {braces}. May be relative.

__schema221.title: description
__schema221.description: An optional description of the server.

__schema222.title: variables
__schema222.description: A map of server variables for URL template substitution.

__schema224.title: ServerVariable
__schema224.description: A server variable for URL template substitution. Use {variableName} in the server URL.

__schema225.title: enum
__schema225.description: An enumeration of valid string values for this variable.

__schema227.title: default
__schema227.description: The default value for substitution if not supplied.

__schema228.title: description
__schema228.description: An optional description for the server variable.

__schema230.title: name
__schema230.description: A unique name to identify the server.

__schema237.title: name
__schema237.description: The name of the parameter. Case-sensitive.

__schema238.title: in
__schema238.description: Location of the parameter. 'path' parameters must have required=true.

__schema239.title: description
__schema239.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema244.description: For query parameters only. Allows empty values (?param=).
__schema244.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema246.title: style
__schema246.description: Serialization style. Defaults vary by 'in': query/cookie='form', path/header='simple'.

__schema247.title: explode
__schema247.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema248.description: For query parameters. When true, allows reserved characters without encoding.
__schema248.title: allowReserved

__schema252.title: examples
__schema252.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema254.title: content
__schema254.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

additionalProperties.title: SecurityScheme
additionalProperties.description: Defines a security scheme that can be used by the operations.

__schema272.title: name
__schema272.description: The name of the header, query, or cookie parameter.

__schema273.title: in
__schema273.description: Location of the API key: 'header' (most common), 'query', or 'cookie'.

__schema274.title: description
__schema274.description: A description of the security scheme.

__schema276.title: HttpSecurityScheme
__schema276.description: HTTP authentication security scheme (Basic, Bearer, etc.).

__schema279.title: bearerFormat
__schema279.description: Hint for bearer token format. Only applicable when scheme='bearer'.

__schema280.title: description
__schema280.description: A description of the security scheme.

__schema289.title: flows
__schema289.description: Allows configuration of the supported OAuth Flows.

__schema291.title: OAuthFlow
__schema291.description: Configuration details for a supported OAuth Flow.

__schema292.title: authorizationUrl
__schema292.description: The authorization URL to be used for this flow.

__schema293.title: tokenUrl
__schema293.description: The token URL to be used for this flow.

__schema294.title: refreshUrl
__schema294.description: The URL to be used for obtaining refresh tokens.

__schema295.title: scopes
__schema295.description: The available scopes for the OAuth2 security scheme.

__schema302.title: device
__schema302.description: Configuration for the Device Authorization Grant flow.

__schema309.title: openIdConnectUrl
__schema309.description: OpenID Connect URL to discover OAuth2 configuration values.

additionalProperties.title: Callback
additionalProperties.description: A map of possible out-of-band callbacks related to the parent operation.

__schema315.title: Callback
__schema315.description: A map of possible out-of-band callbacks related to the parent operation.

__schema317.title: PathItem
__schema317.description: Describes the operations available on a single path.

__schema324.title: Operation
__schema324.description: Describes a single API operation on a path.

__schema325.title: tags
__schema325.description: A list of tags for API documentation grouping.

__schema327.title: summary
__schema327.description: A short summary of the operation. Keep under ~120 characters.

__schema328.title: description
__schema328.description: A verbose description. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema329.title: operationId
__schema329.description: Unique identifier for the operation. Used for code generation.

__schema330.title: parameters
__schema330.description: Parameters for this operation, combined with path-level parameters.

__schema338.description: Marks this operation as deprecated. Defaults to false.

__schema339.title: security
__schema339.description: Security requirements for this operation. Overrides root-level security.

__schema340.title: SecurityRequirement
__schema340.description: Lists the required security schemes for this operation.

__schema355.title: additionalOperations
__schema355.description: Custom HTTP methods beyond the standard ones.
